text: To reduce the risk of respiratory infection and pneumonia where medical care is delayed, responders should:

options.text: Have the patient breathe deeply and cough.
Provide the patient with adequate hydration.
Position the patient in the position of comfort.
Ensure the patient is kept warm and at rest.

explanation: Encouraging a patient to breathe deeply and cough regularly helps clear pulmonary secretions and prevents the collapse of lung tissue (atelectasis). This is a critical nursing intervention to minimize the risk of pneumonia in patients during prolonged transport or delayed medical care.
